name: Hoax.ai Claim and Source Traceability Policy
governing_principle: No claim without traceability. No source without support scope. A reference asset must make its claims inspectable before it asks to be trusted.

governance_claim.traceability: decision log, policy, standard, audit, registry

repository_supported_claim.traceability: existing repository file or data record
repository_supported_claim.must_not: claim external-world facts without external source

future_capability_claim.must_not: imply active public service or tool

source_support_scope_rules: Each source must declare what it supports
Internal governance sources support governance and operational claims only
Internal registry sources must not support broad external factual claims
External sources must be registered before supporting external factual claims
Source scope must be narrow enough to avoid overclaiming
support_location_rules: support_location must exist for supported claims
Source IDs must exist in source-registry
public_claim_mapping_rules: Public claim-bearing statements map to evidence ledger IDs
Future public pages require public claim mapping before release
Public mapped claims must not imply active tool, upload, scoring, or deployment completion
prohibited_claim_patterns: artifact risk converted into subject accusation
validation_requirements: Every ledger claim mapped in claim-source-map
Public claims map to active ledger entries
No external_factual_claim without registered external source
